blocks.content: Congratulations on completing this journey! Job well done!

We hope you've enjoyed traveling with us as you:

- Understood the value of dashboard variables for creating flexible, user-controlled dashboards
- Created query-based variables that populate filter options dynamically from data sources
- Created custom variables with manually defined option lists
- Referenced variables in panel queries to enable dynamic filtering
- Chained variables together to create cascading filter relationships
- Configured variable display options for optimal user experience
- Tested variable interactions across multiple scenarios and edge cases

---

### Related paths

Consider taking the following paths after you complete this journey:

- [Transform data in a Grafana Cloud dashboard](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/data-transformation/)
- [Visualize metrics](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/visualization-metrics/)
- [Visualize logs](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/visualization-logs/)
- [Visualize traces](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/visualization-traces/)

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [Multi-property variables](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/visualizations/dashboards/variables/add-template-variables/#multi-property-query-variables)
- [Multi-property custom variables](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/visualizations/dashboards/variables/add-template-variables/#multi-property-custom-variables)
- [Multi-property query variables](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/visualizations/dashboards/variables/add-template-variables/#multi-property-query-variables)
